Why Free Feats?

It might seem unusual that fusions receive not one but eventually three free combined feats over time, for each character in the fusion, granting them powerful benefits. However, these free combined feats help to offset some of the logistical challenges of having multiple players needing to work together using a single body, including having to coordinate with other players and potentially having characters who would prefer to be positioned in different places on the battlefield forced to share the same position. If your group has an exceptionally high degree of teamwork and coordination and finds sharing a body to be an overall advantage on its own (for instance, building to ensure all members of the fusion work best at the same range while skillfully using combined movement on a high-movement character to offset Speed penalties to other characters), you might consider removing the free combined feats for your group. This is a bit more likely if a single player controls all the characters in a fusion.

Even in the case of a highly coordinated party, it’s usually more fun to be able to use a few combined actions without costing all the ancestry feats for multiple characters, so if you decide to remove the free combined feats for fusion characters, consider using the ancestry paragon variant for every character in the group as a way to level the playing field while giving the fusion characters more space to learn combination abilities!

Orthrus and Beyond

The two-headed giant, cerberus, and hydra are each designed specifically for two, three, and four characters respectively, and their combined actions are balanced around those numbers. However, it’s possible that your group really wants to play a hydra or giant with three heads, or a two-headed canine (often called an orthrus instead of a cerberus) for thematic or aesthetic reasons. In that case, you can adjust existing abilities to work for a different number of characters or reflavor them to work for a different heritage.

Quintron

A Farewell to Arms

The heritages all have something that can work as arms to allow you to play a fusion character with your group regardless of what class and build everyone in the fusion is playing, rather than being stuck with spellcasters, monks, and other classes that can handle things better without any hands. However, if you and your fellow fusion players would rather, you can remove these appendages.

Combined Feat Power and Niche

Combined feats require every character in the fusion to choose the feat, so they come at a higher cost than normal ancestry feats to gain, in addition to the action cost to use them requiring multiple characters’ effort. As such, they tend to have some effects that are especially powerful. For instance, Omega Beam might seem like the damage number is out of control if viewed without context. But on the other hand, four characters had to work together to create the Omega Beam, and there was time when it was charging up to disrupt it or move away. Combined activities are balanced in this way, but by combining into one roll, they can be quite feast or famine. This is part of the reason that they have unusual critical success and critical failure entries, rather than using basic saves, to help avoid a situation where the entire party is looking at double or nothing.

Remember the Legends!.name: 铭记传奇！ Remember the Legends!
Remember the Legends!.text:
摘自艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯的档案

愿你见信安好！我不久将踏上一段艰难的旅程，去发掘远古过去的一些秘密。但在我出发前，我想将我对传奇生物的研究托付于你，让你在不太可能发生的灾害之时也能将其妥善保管。我无法知晓空间元素的召唤为我准备了什么，因此我始终感激你给予的友谊与忠告。我知道我可以信任你。随信附上的是我对并非十二、而是十四种传奇现世的研究。我们必须理解它们的真实，而非执着于我们故事里所持有的朦胧形象——那些形象有时被诗意镀金，有时又被鲜血涂抹。

很抱歉要这样请求你，但我需要你的帮助让它们的故事为人所知。若你能联系你的熟人并分享我寄给你的内容，那么无论接下来发生什么，我都会心满意足。我真的相信，唯有汇聚众人之力，我们才能克服那些“谜团”所准备的一切！

天使在我们的神话与传说中以神圣信使而闻名，但真正理解天使“堕落”意味着什么的人却不多。我常听人使用这个词，仿佛那就表示天使变得邪恶，甚至成了某种类似魔鬼的恶魔；但事实上，天使堕落的原因要多样得多，而且远非所有堕落者都会坠得那么彻底，以至于只能在地狱的深坑中立足。那些行走于凡世的天使必须隐秘行事，以同时避开无知的反应与因天堂遗弃而胆壮的仇敌之攻击。

树灵族是森林之民——我的意思是，它们体现了森林本身，由树木与葱茏植被塑成。多数情况下，它们以行事缓慢而闻名，在做出决定前会思量情势的每一种可能面向。但当它们挚爱的林地陷入危险时，它们也能采取迅捷而凶猛的行动，以阻止最可怕的灾害降临家园。

食脑体是寄生鹦鹉螺生物的一种异常结果：这种生物通常通过操控其他生物的身体行动，而当它与宿主结合在一起时，会形成一种新的共生整体。它们拥有强大的心灵力量，且如其名所示，它们以大脑为食。这并不意味着它们一概都是虐杀成性的凶手，但即便如此，它们不同寻常的饮食有时仍会将它们引向骇人的极端。

魔鬼在世间被广泛认作由地狱那恶意秩序所孕育的恶魔；而事实上，几乎没有魔鬼能够——或愿意——逃离那炼狱帝国的发条与齿轮式役使。对多数魔鬼而言，失去其在等级体系中的位置是不可想象的，那会让它们迷惘而颓丧。但也有一些将它们与地狱的炼狱契约视作枷锁；若它们设法摧毁地狱所持有的那份契约副本，便能挣脱轭缚，穿行多元宇宙追寻各自的目标。诚然，这将以失去其地位所赋予的力量为代价，但为自由付出这样的价格实在轻而易举。

邪眼是一种族裔，其特征是一颗单一的巨眼，占据了它们圆形身体的大部分；它们以伸出的柄足来行走、游泳、拿取物体等等。就连“邪眼”这个名字以及该族裔的起源也饱受争议，每个个体邪眼往往都偏好自己所钟爱的族裔名称与起源故事。

融合体是我在此收录的族裔中，最可能遭到正统学者反对的一类：它们的诸多传承彼此差异太大，以至于许多人声称它们应当各自算作独立族裔。然而我将它们归为一组，原因很简单：这些融合体是格式塔整体——整体大于部分之和；但同时，每一个个别部分也都是一个人，拥有完全独立的心智、认知与独立且同步行动的能力。这意味着，一个融合体事实上不止一个人；对我们这些恰好仅是一个人的人而言，这是一道令人眩晕的哲学结。

石像鬼是夜之守卫，会以石雕像的伪装隐藏自身。尽管常见传说有时会聚焦于最残酷的石像鬼之恶行，但许多石像鬼其实忠诚而坚毅，且有强烈的社群意识。若能越过石像鬼的外表并接纳它们的真实模样，那么与一群同住的石像鬼同盟将会为任何定居点带来巨大的裨益。

巨人是超乎凡俗之人，体现自然的诸多要素与力量的象征；并且它们会随着自身功绩的荣光与故事的传颂而变得更加庞大。它们会建立由故事与物体组成的收藏，向周围人展示自己的力量，同时追寻各自通往伟大的道路。

魔像裔是活体构装：要么是获得生命并得到灵魂的魔像，要么是通过实验手段，将濒死的身躯与构装外壳结合而得救的活体生物。魔像裔源于独特的过程与奇迹，难以或不可能复制。因此，许多魔像裔试图理解自身存在的意义。

拉弥亚是同名受诅咒传奇女王的后裔。尽管最初的拉弥亚或许犯下滔天罪孽，但惩罚她的神祇将诅咒延续到她无辜的后代身上，实在做得过分——这些后裔被灌注了一种吞食有智生物血肉的冲动。每个拉弥亚如何承受这份诅咒的重量，从否认到接纳皆有，映照着我们对悲恸的理解。

引魂使是亡者的引路人，护送灵魂抵达其应归的来世，帮助迷失的幽魂找到道路，并收割那些依附凡世、恐吓生者的恶意亡灵。但如巫妖这般极度聪慧的不死生物，以及名为萨希尔的叛逆引魂使，都是狡诈的敌人，对炼狱的方法了如指掌。为使其措手不及并避开它们的密探，一些引魂使会与其家园位面——炼狱——相切离，作为自由行动者秘密追求目标。然而一旦成为“切离者”，引魂使便会学习并成长，它们也可能转而选择不同的道路。

斯芬克斯是神秘而爱出谜题的守卫者，守护重要的人物、地点或物体。据远古传说，神祇从石头中雕刻出斯芬克斯的诸多传承；而依不同说法，也可能是每个传承都由不同神祇所造——彼此抄袭对方的作品。斯芬克斯会基于它们认为能作为宇宙重大底层真理之隐喻的著名谜题，在哲学上组织自身，例如时间与万物之无常。

虫群血裔是体内并非由血管与血肉器官构成、而是由无数微小生物的虫群构成之人。它们并非自出生起便如此；转化为虫群血裔的过程可能令人迷惘，甚至造成创伤，因为它们的心智会扩张，以容纳体内的芸芸众生。经验丰富的虫群血裔能以多种方式操控自身虫群，在战斗中协助自己，甚至偶尔使自身离散无形。

最后，兽化人会在动物、人形与混合形态之间变形；它们天生拥有一种内在力量，称之为赋礼，但许多其他族裔却视其为危险的诅咒。

——艾瑟莉娅·尼克斯，空间元素的先驱，怪物的代言人

From the Archives of Aitheria Nyx

I hope this message finds you well! I am poised for a difficult journey soon to discover some of the secrets of the ancient past. But before I go, I wanted to leave my research into creatures of legends in your hands so that you might safeguard it in the unlikely event of a catastrophe. I cannot know what the call of the space element has in store for me, and so I am ever grateful to you for your friendship and advice. I know I can trust you with this. Enclosed, you shall find my research on not twelve but fourteen legends come to life. It is critical that we understand their truths, rather than cling to the hazy images we hold in our tales, sometimes gilded with poetry and other times painted with blood.

I’m sorry to ask this of you, but I need your help to make their stories known. If you could reach out to your contacts and share what I have sent you, then I will be content, whatever comes next. I truly believe that it is only by gathering the strength of many that we will overcome whatever the Enigmas have in store!

Angels are well known across our myths and legends as messengers of the divine, but fewer truly comprehend what it means for an angel to fall. Often, I’ve heard the term used as if that meant the angel was evil, or even some kind of fiend akin to a devil, but in reality, angels fall for much more varied reasons, and not nearly all of them fall with such force that they find their footholds only in the pits of Hell. Those who walk the mortal world must do so in secret, to avoid both ignorant reactions and the attacks of foes emboldened by Heaven’s abandonment.

Arboreals are the people of the forest, by which I mean they embody the forest itself, formed as they are from trees and verdure. In most cases, they are famous for taking things slowly, pondering every possible facet of a situation before making a decision. But when their beloved woodlands are in danger, they are capable of taking swift and ferocious action to prevent the worst possible disaster from befalling their home.

Cerebrophages are the unusual result of a parasitic nautilus creature that normally puppets other creatures’ bodies bonding together with its host to form a new symbiotic whole. They have substantial psychic power, and as their name implies, they eat brains. This doesn’t mean they’re unilaterally sadistic killers, but even so, their unusual diet sometimes leads them to gruesome extremes.

Devils are known across the world as fiends born of Hell’s malevolent order, and in truth, few if any escape serving as cogs in the clockwork of the infernal empire, nor would they wish to do so. To most devils, losing their place in that hierarchy would be unthinkable and would leave them lost and listless. But there are others who see their infernal contract with Hell as nothing short of chains, and if they manage to destroy Hell’s copy, they can free themselves from their yoke to pursue their own various goals across the multiverse. True, this comes at the loss of the power their station granted, but such a price is easy to pay for their freedom.

Evil Eyes are an ancestry characterized by a single giant eyeball, which takes up most of their round bodies, with stalks coming off that they use to walk, swim, hold objects, and more. Even the name “evil eye” and the ancestry’s origin are hotly contested, with individual evil eyes each preferring their own favored ancestry name and origin story.

Fusions are the ancestry I’ve included here that might see the most pushback from orthodox scholars, as the various heritages are so different that many claim that they are each a separate ancestry instead. However, I’ve grouped them together for a simple fact: these fusions are gestalts, where the whole is greater than the sum of its parts, yet each individual part is also a person with an entirely separate mind, cognition, and ability to act independently and simultaneously. That means that a single fusion is, in fact, more than one person, which is a dizzying philosophical knot to untangle for those of us who happen to be a single person.

Gargoyles are guardians of the night who hide in the guise of stone statues. While the common tales sometimes focus on the misdeeds of the cruelest gargoyles, many are loyal and stalwart defenders with a strong sense of community. A gargoyle clutch living alongside them is a substantial boon to any settlement, as long as they can see past the gargoyles’ appearances and accept them for who they are.

Giants are people who are larger than life, embodying various elements of nature and symbols of strength, and they grow larger still through the glory of their deeds and the telling of their tales. They build collections of stories and objects that show off their strength to those around them, as they pursue their individual paths to greatness.

Golemborn are living constructs, either golems who have come to life and gained a soul or living creatures saved through experimental means by combining their failing body with a construct shell. Golemborn arise from unique processes and miracles, which are difficult or impossible to replicate. As a result, many seek to understand the meaning of their existence.

Lamias are the offspring of an accursed legendary queen of the same name. While the original Lamia might have committed great sins, the gods who punished her went much too far in passing down their curse to her innocent descendants, imbuing them with an urge to devour the flesh of sapient creatures. How each individual lamia handles the weight of the curse ranges from denial to acceptance, mirroring our understanding of grief.

Psychopomps are the guides of the dead, ushering souls to their proper afterlife, helping lost spirits find their way, and reaping those malevolent undead who cling to the mortal world and terrorize the living. But extremely intelligent undead like liches, as well as rebel psychopomps called sahkils, are wily foes who understand Purgatory’s methods all too well. In order to surprise them and evade their spies, some psychopomps are Severed from their home plane of Purgatory and act as free agents to pursue their goals in secret. Once they become Severed, however, psychopomps learn and grow, and they might choose to follow a different path instead.

Sphinxes are enigmatic, riddling guardians of important people, places, or objects. According to ancient legends, the gods carved the various heritages of sphinxes from stone; depending on who you ask, it might even have been different gods for each heritage, each plagiarizing the other’s work. Sphinxes organize themselves philosophically based on famous riddles they believe act as metaphors for major underlying truths about the universe, such as time and the transience of all things.

Swarmbloods are people whose inner body is made up not of vessels of blood and fleshy organs but instead of swarms of tiny creatures. They don’t start life that way, and their conversion into a swarmblood can be disorienting or even traumatic, as their mind expands to contain the multitudes within them. Experienced swarmbloods can control their swarm to assist them in combat through various means and even discorporate themselves on occasion.

Lastly, werecreatures transform between animal, humanoid, and hybrid forms, born with a power within them that they call a gift but many other ancestries consider a dangerous curse.

— Aitheria Nyx, Herald of the Space Element, Advocate of Monsters

Giantblood (Versatile Heritage).name: 巨人血脉（多用传承） Giantblood (Versatile Heritage)
Giantblood (Versatile Heritage).text:
巨人血裔是与巨人有着强烈联系的其他族裔成员：可能是因为其家族谱系中有巨人血脉、他们自身那些非凡壮举所带来的力量，或来自其他源头。若你想扮演一名拥抱伟大命运的角色，你就该选择巨人血裔。

你可能会……

  • 把自己的丰功伟绩讲给任何愿意听的人
  • 寻觅大胆且可靠的盟友，与你一同踏上下一段宏大的征途。
  • 制定无数策略，以在一个为更小体型之人打造的世界中行动自如。

其他人多半会……

  • 期待你完成传奇专长。
  • 承认并尊重你的肉体力量，同时可能低估你的狡黠。
  • 除非他们确信你是盟友，否则会被你的身材所震慑。

外貌描述

除了极少数例外，巨人血裔在同族裔成员中都高耸如塔。只有那些源自本就巨大的族裔的巨人血裔，或是生活在巨人之中的巨人血裔，才有机会避免成为房间里最大的一位。巨人血裔的体态往往与其非巨人的亲族相似，包括保留其族裔典型的比例与基础外形特征。然而，他们的巨人传承通常会以有趣而不寻常的方式影响其体色。由于许多人形族裔都有皮肤与毛发，各传承的特性描述都会包含这些；但源自鳞片或羽毛族裔的巨人血裔则会拥有相应特征，而更为异类的族裔甚至可能在其所拥有的任何身体部位上显现迹象，例如史莱姆的胶质形态或鬼火那转瞬即逝的光芒。

云血裔——与云巨人及风元素相连的巨人血裔——往往有淡蓝色皮肤。他们的头发通常为白色，蓬松、轻柔如絮，或两者兼具，宛如积云或卷云。特别暴怒的云血裔可能拥有深灰色头发，几乎呈铁砧状，类似积雨云的外观。

霜血裔——与霜巨人及冰元素相连的巨人血裔——常有冷色调的皮肤，呈蓝色或灰色的不同色度。他们的头发常带有白色斑块或条纹，并像被彻底冻结般粘结在一起。

火血裔——与火巨人及火元素相连的巨人血裔——常有暖色调的皮肤，呈红、橙或桃色的不同色度。当他们兴奋或愤怒时，头发会像活焰般舞动。尽管外观如此，这些头发并不会燃烧，而且它仍是实体物体，而非无形的火焰。有时火血裔会用自己的头发制作饰品装点自己或挚爱之人，以便更好地营造出披覆活焰的震撼形象。

丘血裔——与丘巨人相连、且不与任何元素有特别关联的巨人血裔——只是以更大的尺度呈现其族裔同胞的模样。他们拥有与亲族相同类型的肤色与发色。他们往往异常宽阔而健壮，再加上身高，形成极具压迫感的存在。

石血裔——与石巨人及土元素相连的巨人血裔——常有灰色调皮肤，质地粗糙。他们的头发要么稀疏斑驳，要么完全没有，并且常以驼背姿态站立。他们狭窄的骨架覆满坚韧的肌肉，身体往往异常结实。

暴风血裔——与暴风巨人及电元素相连的巨人血裔——常有深蓝或紫色皮肤与发达肌肉。他们拥有深色头发，当情绪强烈波动时，眼睛会闪烁银光。

社会

总体而言，巨人血裔可依据他们何时以及如何成长为如此强大的体型而分成三大类。

巨人裔源自巨人血脉，这种联系在他们出生后不久便显露无遗。他们可能是某位巨人的子女或孙辈，因此可能在成长过程中听闻远古传奇先祖的故事。巨人裔在出生时的体型已接近其主要族裔常见范围的上限，并以惊人的速度成长。年幼的巨人裔很容易打坏东西，因为他们的力量增长快于对四肢的掌控。若在非巨人族群中长大，巨人裔常被误认为比实际年龄大得多，尤其是那些出生于小型或微型父母的巨人裔。而由于他们天生热爱大胆的冒险，巨人裔通常不会纠正那些以为他们已经足够年长、足够负责、可以承担危险任务的人。相反，若在巨人之中长大，巨人裔则比同龄巨人同伴略小。孩童时期，他们常努力融入其中并证明自己与任何人一样强大。在迈向成年的旅途中，他们通常会学会欣赏自身混血传承的益处。

传奇承载者是因其事迹之力而在体型上不断增长的显赫人物。他们在民众心中与想象里化身为一种标志性角色——可能是被赞颂的英雄、遭憎恶的恶徒、反复无常的诡计家，或是开创新路的贤者。化身为巨人既需要伟大成就，也需要极高声望，但仅凭这两点仍不足够。研究巨人族类的学者们争论究竟是什么让这种蜕变成为可能，而对该议题最感兴趣者之一，往往正是传奇承载者本人。变形后不久，许多传奇承载者会出现模糊、如梦似幻的记忆，仿佛自己经历过某种试炼；然而试图记录试炼内容的努力总会让记忆如雾般消散。巨人普遍相信那试炼是神圣的考验，用以验证是否配得上披戴巨人之衣钵，但他们在究竟由谁或什么施加挑战这一点上意见不一。

并非所有在后天获得巨人体型的人都只能思索这些谜团。对于最后一类巨人血裔——继承者——其新得伟力的源头清晰可见。他们吸收了一股力量之泉后，会昏迷一段时间。在身体挣扎求生之际，会将这股力量内化并重塑，使体型增长以容纳之。继承者可能是与巨人战斗并获胜、继而沐浴其血的战士；可能是握住一片神力碎片的探险家；也可能是夺取一把传奇武器的英雄。这种转变往往不止影响外表：新形成的巨人血裔通常会遗忘过去的一些技能，却会立刻获得新的能力。

无论起源如何，几乎所有巨人血裔都与巨人一样，传统上热衷于建立自身名声。他们设定崇高目标，并不惜一切代价去实现。一路上获得的每一道伤疤、每一件战利品与每一个故事，都是其活传奇的一部分，因此经常证明自己是他们生活中不可或缺的要素。巨人血裔常有强烈的内在驱动力想要不断成长，但他们也面临显著的外部压力：巨人裔背负社区的期待；传奇承载者可能害怕一旦淡出视野便失去力量；而继承者则被新披上的强大衣钵与随之而来的沉重期望推着继续前行。

信念

多数巨人血裔相信自己被标记为非凡之人——无论他们是天生注定走向史诗命运，还是因自身事迹而被选中。自信甚至爱夸口十分常见，但傲慢并非普遍存在。毕竟，许多传奇都因狂妄而戛然而止。有些巨人血裔拥有如巨人般的自我膨胀，也有一些更脚踏实地。

更务实的巨人血裔通常不觉得粉碎对手有什么可羞耻的，也不在意是否需要一场公平对决。他们宁愿挑选细节来把自己描绘得光彩夺目，也不愿不必要地冒致命风险。即便如此，当需要为所信之事而战时，巨人血裔依旧是可怕的战斗者。许多巨人血裔相信，当他们毫无保留之时，其信念的原初力量足以在形而上层面扭曲现实，使之偏向自己。这一哲学可能源于与巨人的接触——巨人相信自己是神性存在——或源于巨人血裔在逆境中屡战屡胜的经历。

巨人血裔比巨人更可能虔信宗教。各谱系的巨人血裔都可能崇拜与故事和传奇相关的神祇。在阿拉卡尔，他们可能敬奉三则故事的成员（英雄贾塔、恶徒苏马沃罗、或诡计家塞瓦），甚至崇拜命运本身。他们也可能选择与其谱系所承袭的巨人传承文化根源相关的神祇：例如云血裔偏好艺术之神祇，火血裔偏好熔炉之神祇，霜血裔偏好战争之神祇，丘血裔偏好狂欢或战斗之神祇，石血裔偏好自然之神祇，而暴风血裔偏好海洋与天空之神祇。

常见典范 发展你的传奇，改变世界，证明你的价值

常见禁忌 安于现状，为安抚他人而让自己变得渺小，放弃

姓名

源自巨人的巨人血裔会得到冗长而沉重的名字，以契合其社区对其未来抱持的宏大期望。那些在后天因事迹或经历而获得巨人之力的人，往往仍保有其出身族裔常见的姓名。这类人通常勇敢无畏，往往在获得巨人之力之前就已能赢得头衔。这种模式让一些巨人哲人不禁自问：这些英勇之人究竟是被潜意识里沉睡的、终将化为巨人的潜能所驱使，还是说巨人的转化会寻觅并加冕真正的值得之人。

示例姓名

Isbadaden, Kathoron, Trelunkut, Cruvoxan, Thallas, Wurva, Gluun

示例头衔

The Steel Tower, Titanslayer, Lord of the Inferno, Throatripper, Miracle Medic, Peacemaker

Giantbloods are members of other ancestries with strong connections to giants, whether due to a giant in their family tree, the power of their own larger-than-life accomplishments, or another source. If you want to play a character who embraces a great destiny, you should play a giantblood.

You Might...

  • Share your accomplishments with anyone willing to listen
  • Seek out daring and capable allies to join you on your next grand venture.
  • Develop numerous strategies for navigating a world built for smaller people.

Others Probably...

  • Expect you to achieve legendary feats.
  • Acknowledge and respect your physical strength, while potentially underestimating your cunning.
  • Are intimidated by your stature unless they’re certain that you’re an ally.

Physical Description

With very few exceptions, giantbloods tower over other members of their ancestry. Only giantbloods descended from massive ancestries, or those who live among giants, have a chance to avoid being the largest one in the room. The shape of a giantblood’s body often resembles their non-giant relatives, including retaining their ancestry’s typical proportions and base physical features. However, their giant heritage typically influences their coloration in interesting and unusual ways. Since many humanoid ancestries have skin and hair, the feature descriptions for each heritage include them, but giantbloods descended from scaly or feathery ancestries have those features instead, and ancestries even further afield might have signs on whatever body parts they possess, such as a slime’s gelatinous form or a will-o’-wisp’s ephemeral light.

Cloudbloods, giantbloods with a connection to cloud giants and the element of air, tend to have pale blue skin. Their hair is typically white and either fluffy, wispy, or both, like a cumulus or cirrus cloud. Particularly wrathful cloudbloods might have dark gray hair almost shaped like an anvil, akin to the appearance of a cumulonimbus cloud.

Frostbloods, giantbloods with a connection to frost giants and the element of ice, often have cool-toned skin in shades of blue or gray. Their hair often has white patches or streaks that stick together as if frozen solid.

Firebloods, giantbloods with a connection to fire giants and the element of fire, often have warm-toned skin in shades of red, orange, or peach. Their hair dances like living flames when they are excited or angry. Despite its appearance, this hair doesn’t burn, and it’s still a solid object rather than an intangible flame. Sometimes firebloods decorate themselves or their loved ones with accessories made from their own hair, the better to give off the impressive appearance of being clad in living flames.

Hillbloods, giantbloods connected to hill giants and without a particular connection to one of the elements, resemble other members of their ancestry at a larger scale. They have the same sorts of skin and hair colors as their relatives. They tend to be exceptionally broad and heavyset, which, combined with their height, makes for an intimidating presence.

Stonebloods, giantbloods with a connection to stone giants and the element of earth, often have gray-toned skin with a rough texture. Their hair is either patchy or nonexistent, and they stand with a stooped posture. Their narrow frames are covered in wiry muscles, and their bodies tend to be exceptionally solid.

Stormbloods, giantbloods with a connection to storm giants and the element of electricity, often have dark blue or purple skin and prominent muscles. They have dark hair, and their eyes flash silver when they experience strong emotions.

Society

Broadly speaking, giantbloods can be divided into three different categories based upon when and how they grew to their mighty size.

Giantkin are descended from giants, a connection that becomes evident soon after they are born. They might be the child or grandchild or a giant, and so they might grow up hearing tales of legendary ancestors from the distant past. While giantkins’ size at birth is on the upper end of the typical range for their primary ancestry, they grow at a formidable pace. Young giantkin are prone to breaking things, as their strength outpaces their control over their limbs. When raised among non-giants, giantkin are often mistaken for being far older than they actually are, especially those born to Small or Tiny parents. And with their natural love for daring adventure, giantkin are unlikely to correct anyone who assumes that they’re old enough and responsible enough to handle a perilous quest. When raised among giants, on the other hand, giantkin are a bit smaller than their peers. As children, they often strive to blend in and prove that they are just as mighty as everyone else. On their journey to adulthood, they typically learn to appreciate the benefits of their blended heritage.

Legendbearers are prominent figures who grow in size from the force of their deeds. They embody an iconic role in the hearts and minds of the people, whether they are lauded heroes, despised villains, mercurial tricksters, or innovative sages. Transforming into a giant requires both great accomplishments and great renown, but these two factors alone aren’t enough. Scholars of giantkind debate what makes this metamorphosis possible, and few are as interested in the topic as legendbearers themselves. Soon after the transformation, many legendbearers experience faint, dreamlike memories of undergoing some sort of trial, though efforts to record the trial’s contents inevitably cause the memories to dissolve into mist. Giants widely believe that the trial is a divine test for worthiness to assume a giant’s mantle, though they disagree on who or what administers the challenges.

Not all who acquire a giant’s stature later in life are left to ponder such mysteries. For the last giantblood category, inheritors, the source of their newfound might is abundantly clear. After absorbing a wellspring of power, they fall unconscious for a time. As their body struggles to survive, it internalizes and reshapes the force, growing in size to contain it. Inheritors might be warriors who defeat giants in combat and bathe in their blood, explorers who grasp a shard of divine power, or heroes who claim a legendary weapon. The transformation often goes beyond their external features, with the newly formed giantblood typically forgetting some of their previous skills while instantly acquiring new abilities.

Regardless of their origins, almost all giantbloods share a giant’s traditional love for building their reputation. They set lofty goals and stop at nothing to achieve them. Each of the scars, trophies, and stories they acquire along the way is a piece of their living legend, and so regularly proving themselves is an essential part of their lives. While giantbloods often have a strong intrinsic motivation to keep growing, they also face significant external pressure as well. Giantkin bear the weight of their community’s expectations, legendbearers might fear losing their power if they fade into the background, and inheritors are spurred onward by the might of their new mantle and all the heavy expectations that come with it.

Beliefs

Most giantbloods believe that they have been marked for greatness, whether they were born into an epic destiny or chosen for their deeds. Boastful confidence is commonplace, but arrogance is far from universal. After all, many are the legends that are cut short by hubris. While some giantbloods have the egos of giants, others are a bit more grounded in reality.

These more practical giantbloods usually see no shame in crushing an adversary rather than ensuring a fair fight. They’d rather cherry-pick details to paint themselves in the best light than risk mortal danger unnecessarily. Still, when it comes time to fight for what they believe in, giantbloods are formidable combatants. Many giantbloods believe that when they hold nothing back, the raw force of their convictions is strong enough to metaphysically bend reality in their favor. This philosophy might arise from contact with giants, who believe themselves to be divine beings, or else from the giantblood’s history of triumph over adversity.

Giantbloods are more likely than giants to be religious. Giantbloods of all lineages might worship deities tied to stories and legends. On Alacar, they might venerate members of the Three Stories (Jata the Hero, Sumaworo the Villain, or Sewa the Trickster) or even Fate itself. They might choose deities tied to the cultural traditions of their lineage’s giant heritage, like deities of art for cloudblood, the forge for fireblood, warfare for frostblood, revelry or battle for hillblood, nature for stoneblood, or the sea and sky for stormblood.

Popular Edicts grow your legend, change the world, prove your worth

Popular Anathemas be complacent, make yourself small to placate others, give up

Names

Giantbloods who are descended from giants receive long and weighty names befitting the grand expectations that their communities hold for their futures. Those who gain the might of giants later in life from deeds or experiences often have names typical for their ancestry of origin. Such individuals tend to be brave and bold, the sort to earn titles even before they gain giant powers. This pattern leads some giant philosophers to ask themselves whether these valiant individuals are driven by some subconscious dormant potential to become a giant, or whether the giant transformation finds and anoints the worthy.

Sample Names

Isbadaden, Kathoron, Trelunkut, Cruvoxan, Thallas, Wurva, Gluun

Sample Titles

The Steel Tower, Titanslayer, Lord of the Inferno, Throatripper, Miracle Medic, Peacemaker

Giantblood have only a few ancestry feats uniquely available only to them, but they unlock the potential to the vast majority of giant ancestry feats, listed below in Additional Feats. Additional feats with a heritage prerequisite require a giantblood to take the associated lineage feat.

Overmind Archetype.name: 超脑变体 Overmind Archetype
Overmind Archetype.text:
当一只食脑者的心智扩展到远超寻常极限时——通常要么源于强烈的心智训练，要么因为吞噬了无数非凡的大脑——这只食脑者便会蜕变为一种更强大的变体，被称为“超脑”。食脑者超脑拥有强大的灵能能力，以及格外巨大且扭曲的头颅，仿佛它们庞大的大脑几乎要从内部向外爆裂而出。确实，有时超脑的大脑会真的清晰可见，因为它已逃离了超脑头后方鹦鹉螺壳的束缚。

超脑感知这个世界时，会将其视作叠加在一个认知领域之上：一种夹层心景，位于凡人领域与星界位面之间的半途之处，其中包含着各种生物的思维形态。由于它们杂糅而庞大的大脑能够同时计算多股思绪流，超脑能够在战斗或其他紧张的遭遇中将自身居中至不同的心境框架，使它们能依据自己与认知领域不同性相的连结，采用多种强大且有益的技法。

When a cerebrophage's mind expands far past the ordinary limits, typically either thanks to intense mental training or due to devouring numerous prodigious brains, the cerebrophage transforms into a more powerful variant know as an overmind. Cerebrophage overminds have powerful psychic abilities and especially large and distorted heads, as if their enormous brains are almost bursting outward from within. Indeed, sometimes an overmind's brain is literally visible, having escaped the confines of the nautilus shell at the back of the overmind's head.

Overminds perceive this world as existing overlayed with a cognitive realm, an interstitial mindscape halfway between the mortal realm and the Astral Plane, which contains the thoughforms of various creatures. Because of their eclectic and oversized brain's ability to calculate multiple streams of thought at the same time, overminds are able to center themselves into different frames of mind during battles or other tense encounters, allowing them to adopt a variety of powerful and beneficial techniques depending on their attachment to different aspects of the cognitive realm.

Diabolic Schemer Archetype.name: 恶魔策谋者变体 Diabolic Schemer Archetype
Diabolic Schemer Archetype.text:
仅仅因为一个无契约的魔鬼逃离了地狱的掌控，并不意味着他们会抛弃那些在仍为地狱压迫性的等级体系效力时引向成功的同样把戏与技巧。相反，他们获得了将自己最喜爱的把戏适应于新目的的自由，甚至还能尝试掌握一些他们从其他魔鬼传承中艳羡的技巧——只要在生理上他们能够做到。

将恶魔策谋家区分开来的，是他们那错综复杂、如迷宫般的阴谋与诡计之精巧本质。任何魔鬼——无契约或其他——都能在一定程度上进行谋划，但恶魔策谋家会把事情提升到新的层次：一层又一层地筹划，设下一个又一个的应急预案，以确保他们的奇谋总能达成预期结果。毕竟，如果你从一开始就操纵了游戏，使所有结果都通向胜利，那你就不可能失败。每当你看到某个无契约的魔鬼夸口说他们那荒唐迂回的计划才完成了四分之一时，他们很可能正是置身于真正史诗级运作中的恶魔策谋家。

作为一名恶魔策谋家，你已解锁用你最聪明、最阴险的计谋来折磨敌人的全部潜能（并在必要时对旁观者或盟友中那些容易得手的目标下手）。当你更深入地发掘恶魔策谋家的力量时，你很快会发现：每学会一种新技巧，都会为你的策略增添一个新的侧面。你先用一个把戏打乱敌人的节奏，随后迅速以另一个计谋或反击接续而上。

地狱训练的报酬十分丰厚；在外人看来，你的各种把戏与计谋或许多样而彼此不相干，但它们最终都指向同一个结果：让你的敌人陷入无路可走的境地。真正的恶魔策谋家典范，袖中总藏着针对任何情况的一个反应动作（），并且还拥有一两种狡诈的攻击，用以进一步将胜算向自己倾斜。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-living-legends-pf2e.yol-feats.Item.5vkD82M1wnXa8T6C]{Always be Scheming}

Just because a contractless devil has escaped from under Hell’s thumb doesn’t mean they abandon the same tricks and techniques that led them to success back when they still served Hell’s oppressive hierarchy. If anything, they gain the freedom to adapt their favorite tricks to new purposes and even try to master a few techniques they envy from other devil heritages, as long as it’s physically possible for them to perform.

What sets a diabolic schemer aside is the elaborate nature of their labyrinthine plots and schemes. Any devil, contractless or otherwise, can plan to a certain extent, but diabolic schemers take things to the next level, plotting layer after layer and contingency after contingency to ensure that their gambits always reach a desired outcome. After all, you can’t lose if you rig the game from the start so that all outcomes lead to victory. Whenever you see a contractless devil bragging that their ridiculously circuitous plan is one quarter complete, they’re likely a diabolic schemer in the midst of some truly epic machinations.

As a diabolic schemer yourself, you’ve unlocked the full potential to bedevil your foes (and any easy marks among bystanders or allies as necessary) with your cleverest and most fiendish schemes. As you delve deeper into the power of the diabolic schemer, you quickly discover that each new technique learned adds another facet to your strategies. You start with a trick to throw off your foe’s rhythm and quickly follow up with another scheme or counterattack.

The wages of Hell’s training are plentiful, and to an outside observer, it might seem like your various tricks and schemes are varied and disparate, but they all circle back toward leaving your foes in a no-win situation. A true paragon of a diabolic schemer has a reaction for any circumstance up their sleeve, as well as a tricky attack or two they can use to throw the odds even further in their own favor.

Agent of the Afterlife Archetype.name: 来世使者变体 Agent of the Afterlife Archetype
Agent of the Afterlife Archetype.text:
尽管已被断离，大多数作为独立代理在凡人世界游荡的灵魂引渡者，仍会以自己的方式珍视他们与炼狱的旧日联系。对许多人而言，他们的断离是一种必要之恶；这是一场悲剧，但为了让他们履行伟大的职责，这样的代价仍是值得的。随着时光流逝，一些断离者在发展出情感丰富的内心生活与个体性后，开始以不同的方式看待这一切——这些变化使他们离“炼狱代理人”的角色越来越远。

然而，能够设法解开重获旧日力量秘密的，不仅是那些渴望重燃昔日威能的人；同样也包括那些更希望成为全新存在的人。那些触及这些力量的灵魂引渡者，会成为强大的断离者，通常被称为“来世代理”，不论他们是否打算继续遵循炼狱的最后命令。

正是朝任一方向的坚定信念，使来世代理得以显现其力量。因此，唯有那些迷失且踌躇不定、尚未决定自己真正是谁或是什么的灵魂引渡者，才永远找不到道路。忠于自己的内心，并看穿他们用以躲避自己与他人的面具，使灵魂引渡者得以直面真实的自我，重拾潜藏其中的真正力量。这力量可能是他们在断离中失去的旧日力量，也可能是唯有选择独特道路、从炼狱分离之后才能领悟的全新力量。正因如此，许多来世代理的能力与灵魂引渡者原本的传承无关，即便这些能力模仿了其他灵魂引渡者所拥有的本领亦然。对于那些通常只对强大的阎魔罗阇死者审判官开放的技艺更是如此——迄今为止，这些审判官要么太过重要，要么过于显眼，以至于从未经历断离。

一些更为独立的来世代理不喜欢用这个名称来指称所有掌握这些灵魂引渡者技艺的大师，因为他们坚持认为自己并非来世或任何更高力量的“代理人”，而是自己命运的主宰。即便如此，虽然他们或许不会接受坟墓或炼狱的祝福，他们的旧日家园仍珍视着他们，并赐予他们选择新生活所需的力量。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-ancestries-living-legends-pf2e.yol-feats.Item.7DI0enrazCsSzCoF]{隐秘命运}

Despite being Severed, most psychopomps who roam the mortal world as independent agents still cherish their former connection to Purgatory in their own way. To many, their Severing was a necessary evil, a tragedy that was worth it to allow them to fulfill their great duty. Over time, some Severed come to see things differently as they develop an emotional inner life and individuality that brings them farther away from their role as an agent of Purgatory.

And yet it is not just those who yearn to rekindle their old power but also those who wish to become something new instead who can manage to unlock the secrets to unlock their old power. Those psychopomps who tap into these powers become mighty Severed known usually as agents of the afterlife, whether they plan on continuing to follow Purgatory’s last orders or not.

The strong convictions in either direction are what allow an agent of the afterlife to manifest their powers. Thus, it’s only those psychopomps who are lost and uncertain, having not yet made up their minds on who or what they truly are, who can never find their way. Sticking true to their own heart and seeing beneath the mask they use to hide from themselves and others allows a psychopomp to face their true selves and recover their true strength hiding within. This might be their old strength that they lost during the Severing, or it could be something brand new that they could have only realized by choosing their unique path and splitting off from Purgatory. As such, many of the capabilities of an Agent of the Afterlife are independent of a psychopomp’s original heritage, even when they mimic abilities found by other psychopomps. This goes doubly for techniques normally only available to the powerful yamaraj judges of the dead, who have thus far proved either too important or too blatant to ever undergo the Severing themselves.

Some of the more independent agents of the afterlife dislike the use of this name to indicate all masters of these psychopomp techniques, as they maintain that they are not “agents” of the afterlife or any other greater force but instead masters of their own destinies. Even so, while they might not accept the grave or Purgatory’s blessings, their old home cherishes them and bestows upon them the power they need to live the new lives they choose.
